name: Universal Field Validator — IDs, codes & patterns
description: Live, as-you-type validation for any field with a structure. Built for participant and specimen IDs with check characters (ISO 7064, Damm, Verhoeff, Luhn — catches virtually every typo and mis-scan, in single fields or pooled several-IDs-in-one-box fields), and equally at home validating ANY text field against a regex — study codes, lab numbers, device serials, legacy IDs — with progressive what's-still-missing guidance while typing, and no administrator-added validation types needed. Per-rule enforcement (message / confirm / block save), a server-side audit of API and import writes, and three ways to configure: the settings dialog (a rule can cover many fields), fast field-name entry, or @UVALIDATE field annotations for bulk setup straight from the data dictionary. No JavaScript Injector required.

authors.name: CHPR
authors.institution: Bamenda Center for Health Promotion and Research (CHPR)

action-tags.description: Validate this Text or Notes field with the Universal Field Validator, from the Online Designer or the data dictionary CSV instead of the module's Configure dialog. Forms: @UVALIDATE (default ISO 7064 Mod 37,36 check), @UVALIDATE=verhoeff (pick an algorithm), or a full-rule JSON such as @UVALIDATE={"algorithm":"none","pattern":"FC[0-9]{4}","blockSave":"hard"} or @UVALIDATE={"type":"pooled","expectedIds":3}. JSON keys: type, algorithm, source, pattern, strip, keepChars, idLengths, idMinLen, idMaxLen, expectedIds, blockSave, note. Fields with identical tags are grouped into one rule; a malformed tag shows a configuration error.

project-settings.name:
Universal Field Validator
Add ONE rule per kind of validation — a rule can cover any number of fields. Three ways to attach fields to a rule: the field picker (click its + to add more fields), the fast-entry box (type field names separated by commas), or skip this dialog entirely and tag fields with @UVALIDATE in the Online Designer's Action Tags box / the data dictionary CSV (bulk setup — see the documentation). Leave a rule's advanced boxes blank to use the defaults.

How to log invalid IDs caught on the server (API / import / JavaScript-off). Every mode except 'off' logs field, instrument, event and instance; the modes differ in how the invalid VALUE and the RECORD ID are stored.

choices.name: Hashed value + raw record ID (default — correlates repeats without storing the invalid ID; the record ID stays readable so staff can fix the record)
Strict: no value, record ID hashed (minimal-identifier mode for sites where record IDs are themselves identifying)
Raw value + raw record ID (only if your data-governance policy allows identifiers in module logs)
Do not log server-side detections at all

sub_settings.name: Rule label (optional — your own name for this rule, e.g. 'Specimen IDs' or 'Legacy screening codes')

choices.name: Pooled (several IDs jammed into one field)

sub_settings.name: Field(s) this rule validates — click + to add more fields to this same rule
Fast entry (optional): more field names for this rule, separated by commas or spaces. Merged with the pickers above; unknown names show a configuration error.
Check-character method — must match how the IDs were minted. Validating a plain format/code with no check character? Pick 'No check character' and set the format pattern below.

choices.name: ISO 7064 Mod 11,2 — digits, check 0-9 or X

sub_settings.name: Optional format pattern (regex for ONE ID). Required if method is 'No check character'. JavaScript regex; avoid nested quantifiers like (a+)+.
Separators to ignore before checking (default: - / space _ | \ )
Pooled only: extra characters to keep when splitting a pooled field (rarely needed)
Pooled only: exact ID length(s), comma-separated (e.g. 10 or 10, 12). Overrides the min/max range below.
Pooled only: minimum ID length (default 8). Used only if exact lengths are blank.
Pooled only: maximum ID length (default 14; must be less than 2× the minimum).
Pooled only: expected number of IDs in the field (optional; warns if different). Positive whole number.
On an invalid ID (client-side; the server always logs — see above)

choices.name: Advisory — warn and ask before saving
Compulsory — block the browser save until fixed
